LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA MOTION TO REMOVE, DISMISS AND BAN USES SUCH AS DURING THE TRIAL THE DECLARATIONS OF (LALEH SHAHIDEH, CHRISTOPHER BROOKS, E.J. SUNG, TERRENCE PARR, SAMI ROLLINS, PATRICIA FRANCIS-LYON AND DAVID EPSTEIN) IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS CITED NO LEGAL BASIS TO REMOVE, DISMISS OR BAN THE DECLARATIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT ON JANUARY 16, 2013. THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF FILING AN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS IN CONNECTION WITH HIS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE REQUEST OF FRENCH TO ENGLISH AND ENGLISH TO FRENCH INTERPRETATION SERVICE AT THE HEARING IS ALSO DENIED. THE COURT HAS NO LEGAL DUTY TO PROVIDE A COURT INTERPRETER IN THIS CASE, AND IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE IS INCAPABLE OF UNDERSTANDING THE ENGLISH LANGUAGE OR IS INCAPABLE OF EXPRESSING HIMSELF IN THE ENGLISH LANGUAGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/AJR

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE MOTION WAS NOT TIMELY FILED OR SERVED UNDER CCP 1005. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/AJR)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-25-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA'S MOTION TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. IT IS NOT ENTIRELY CLEAR WHAT CHANGES PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO MAKE, THE MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1324, AND ALLOWING AMENDMENT AT THIS STAGE OF THE PROCEEDINGS WOULD BE PREJUDICIAL TO DEFENDANT BECAUSE DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNABLE TO BRING A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION GIVEN THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Ntc Of Motion To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA's Motion To File A First Amended Complaint is denied. It is not entirely clear what changes plaintiff seeks to make, the motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324, and allowing amendment at this stage of the proceedings would be prejudicial to defendant because defendant would be unable to bring a summary judgment motion given the current trial date. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Matter argued. Ruling: PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is DENIED. Order signed. Bruce Highman, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

Noticeof Motion And Motion To Remove, Dismiss And Ban Uses Such As During The Trial The Declarations
Off Calendar Mar-05-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - SORIBA BANGOURA, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA Motion To Remove, Dismiss And Ban Uses Such As During The Trial The Declarations of (Laleh Shahideh, Christopher Brooks, E.J. Sung, Terrence Parr, Sami Rollins, Patricia Francis-Lyon and David Epstein) is Denied. Plaintiff has cited no legal basis to remove, dismiss or ban the declarations filed by Defendant on January 16, 2013. This is without prejudice to Plaintiff filing an opposition to Defendant's motion for summary judgment or objections to the declarations in connection with his opposition to Defendant's motion for summary judgment. The request of French to English and English to French interpretation service at the hearing is also denied. The Court has no legal duty to provide a court interpreter in this case, and in any event, Plaintiff has not shown that he is incapable of understanding the English language or is incapable of expressing himself in the English language. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/AJR

Off Calendar Feb-28-2013 Continued To Mar-12-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: SORIBA BANGOURA, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
STACEY LEASK, ESQ. AND MICHAEL VARTAIN, ESQ., 415.391.1155, FOR DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses IS Continued on the court's own motion to March 12, 2013 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of its moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 4, 2013 at 4 pm, or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. JUDGE PRO TEM NILS ROSENQUEST, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
